title: Fix conductor-poll epic-scoping: count slug-id children (reality-check finding)
description:
conductor-poll.sh under-reports epic progress when children have slug-based ids.

REPRO (from live certify run 2026-07-02): certify created an epic with 3 children via `br create --parent <epic> --slug cert-one ...`. beads assigned the children non-dotted ids (e.g. `<session>-cert-one-xxx`), NOT `<epic>.N`. In conductor-poll.sh the python `in_scope()` matches children with `rid(r).startswith(epic + ".")`, so slug-id children were excluded from `children`; progress.closed stayed 0/3 forever even though the worker had created all files and closed every bead + the parent epic. Consequence: conductor-certify.sh never saw closed>=1, so it NEVER injected the pane-kill, so G7 recovery was never exercised and the run hit TIMEOUT (exit 1) despite the workflow actually succeeding.

FIX: scope children to the epic by the beads parent/epic relationship (the `parent`/`epic_id`/`parent_id` field on each row) instead of (or in addition to) dotted-id prefix. Handle both dotted-id children (`<epic>.N`, as in skills-flywheel-conductor-nme.*) AND slug-id children. Do NOT special-case formats with brittle string ops — key off the actual parent field the beads JSON exposes (inspect `br list --json` / `br show <epic> --json` to confirm the field name).

HARD DELIVERABLE (deterministic): add a regression case to tests/conductor-poll.test.sh with a mocked `br` returning an epic + slug-id children where some are closed; assert progress.closed counts them. Keep existing dotted-id cases green.

Also verify conductor-certify.sh injected-kill path fires once closed>=1 (its `closed>=1` guard depends on this fix). Adjust certify only if the fix surfaces a second assumption there.

Files: skills/engineering/flywheel-conductor/scripts/conductor-poll.sh, skills/engineering/flywheel-conductor/tests/conductor-poll.test.sh (and conductor-certify.sh only if needed). Reserve these via Agent Mail before editing. This BLOCKS the reality-check (.12) and therefore ship (.13).
design:
SECOND defect in the same file (found in live dogfood 2026-07-02) — fix together, one worker, one reservation on conductor-poll.sh:

conductor-poll.sh pane classification misdetects dead panes. When a codex pane self-updates it prints "Please restart Codex" / "Update ran successfully" and drops to a shell prompt like:
  rgbrgy:skills/ (feat/flywheel-conductor*) $                 [20:55:57]
The shell regex re.search(r"(\$|%)\s*$", tail_txt) fails because the line ends with a bracketed timestamp + padding AFTER the prompt char, so the pane is misclassified "idle" instead of "shell"; triage then never raises G7 and dead workers go unrecovered.

FIX: (a) detect shell prompts even with trailing timestamp/padding after the $/% prompt char; (b) recognize the restart/update banner as a distinct dead/needs-restart pane state that conductor-triage.sh escalates to G7. Add deterministic regression fixtures + assertions to tests/conductor-poll.test.sh (and evals/ if used) for BOTH this and the slug-id scoping bug. Keep all existing cases green.
close_reason: Fixed conductor-poll epic scoping for slug-id children via parent/dependents metadata and added dead-pane restart detection regressions.

title: conductor-poll: br list hides closed beads -> progress always 0 (use --all); true root cause behind nme.14
description:
conductor-poll.sh reports progress.closed=0 forever on live swarms because it reads `br --db <db> --allow-stale list --json`, and `br list` EXCLUDES closed beads by default. Verified in the certify sandbox: default `br list --json` -> 0 rows; `br list --all --json` -> 4 rows (all closed). So the moment workers close beads they disappear from the poll input; progress.closed never increments and certify never fires its injected pane-kill (G7 stays unproven). This is the ACTUAL root cause; nme.14 misdiagnosed it as slug-id scoping (real children are dotted `<epic>.N`).

FIX (root cause): in conductor-poll.sh, fetch the base bead set with `br --db "$db" --allow-stale list --all --json` (confirm `--all` is the flag that includes closed via `br list --help`). Keep the epic dependents/parent scoping from nme.14 (harmless, defensive) but the closed-visibility fix is what matters. progress = {closed: children with status==closed, total: all children}.

HARD DELIVERABLE (regression that would have caught this): add a case to tests/conductor-poll.test.sh with a mock `br` whose DEFAULT `list --json` returns only open/in_progress rows but `list --all --json` returns those PLUS closed children; assert progress.closed counts the closed ones and total is correct. This mock divergence is the exact real-world behavior the old fixtures missed.

Also verify conductor-certify.sh now reaches closed>=1 -> injects pane-kill -> G7 respawn -> recovery -> PASS. File: skills/engineering/flywheel-conductor/scripts/conductor-poll.sh + tests. Reserve conductor-poll.sh via Agent Mail before editing.
close_reason: Fixed conductor-poll to read br list --all so closed child beads remain visible in progress accounting; added regression for default-list closed-bead omission.

title: G7 recovery hollow: ntm respawn does not relaunch codex — escalate to kill+fresh-spawn
description:
G7 recovery is hollow: ntm respawn does NOT relaunch codex. PROVEN 2026-07-02: (1) live certify — "G7 detected on pane 1 -> respawning" fired 7x, closed stuck at 1/3, TIMEOUT; (2) direct test — `ntm respawn skills --panes=1 --force` on a RUNNING idle codex pane dropped it to a bare shell with no codex relaunch. ntm respawn uses `tmux respawn-pane -k`, which kills the agent but leaves a shell. certify then `ntm send --cod`s the prompt into a shell (no codex listening), so the pane stays dead and G7 re-fires until timeout. Detection (nme.14) and closed-bead counting (nme.15) are now PROVEN correct — only recovery is broken.

PROVEN-WORKING recovery: `ntm kill <session>` + fresh `ntm spawn <session> --cod=N --init-prompt ...` relaunches codex cleanly; the worker resumes via `br ready` (shared tree + beads make this safe). There is no reliable per-pane relaunch via ntm today.

FIX (worker):
1. conductor-certify.sh G7 handler: after ONE respawn attempt that does not restore the pane (G7 still present on the next poll), ESCALATE to `ntm kill "$session"` + fresh `ntm spawn "$session" --cod=1 --init-prompt "$init_prompt"`; then continue the drive loop (remaining beads still open, worker resumes). Cap escalations (e.g. 1 respawn + 1 kill/respawn) to avoid infinite loops; on exhaustion, fail with a clear message. g7_recovered=true only once closed advances past the pre-kill count.
2. guards.md G7: rewrite the Fix to state ntm respawn alone leaves a bare shell and does NOT relaunch the agent; the reliable recovery is session-level kill+fresh-spawn (note the multi-worker cost; workers resume from br ready), and reference G10 for any in_progress bead left by the killed worker.
3. commands.md respawn recipe + flywheel-local-launcher flywheel-kickoff.sh readiness note: correct the "respawn then re-send prompt" guidance accordingly (respawn does not relaunch; use kill+fresh-spawn).

PROOF: a green `conductor-certify.sh --repo <dir> --yes` run (detection + WORKING recovery + epic reaches 3/3). Reserve conductor-certify.sh, references/guards.md, references/commands.md, and flywheel-local-launcher/scripts/flywheel-kickoff.sh via Agent Mail before editing.

title: V1: Stress-test-before-finalize gate (today-vs-north-star + measurability)
description:
Add an explicit stress-test step at the end of init (and review/update) in WORKFLOW.md, a Quality Bar line in SKILL.md, and Quality Checks + an eval case.

WHY (impact HIGH): the fatal failure is asserting an aspiration as present truth you cannot measure. Current skill has a static Quality Bar line ('durable constraints, not aspiration') but NO active step that tests the drafted VISION.md. Does not overlap check (judges external work) or calibrate (validates check vs history) — those never test the vision doc itself at authoring time.

DESIGN — distinguish, don't delete: a vision legitimately holds a north-star (solution bet, durable advantage). For each load-bearing claim, force it into exactly one of:
- durable constraint / tradeoff -> fine as-is
- explicitly-labelled north-star DIRECTION (not asserted as achieved) -> fine
- present-truth claim -> must be measurable/checkable, or reframe it
Ban only the unmeasurable present-truth assertion.

ACCEPTANCE:
- WORKFLOW.md Init step: 'Stress test before finalizing' with the three-way sort + measurability question ('could someone tell whether this is true today?').
- Same gate referenced from review + update.
- SKILL.md Quality Bar: one line naming the today-vs-north-star + measurability test.
- REFERENCE.md Quality Checks: one bullet.
- evals.json: case that catches an unmeasurable present-truth claim (e.g. 'fastest onboarding in the category' stated as current fact) and reframes/labels it.
close_reason: Implemented: stress-test-before-finalize gate added to WORKFLOW Init step 7 + Review/Update; SKILL Quality Bar line; REFERENCE Stress Test section; eval case added.

title: V3: Canonical-term + plain-language discipline (+ concise Glossary fallback)
description:
Give VISION.md terminology discipline WITHOUT owning a glossary system. Defer to an existing canonical vocabulary source; if none exists, add a concise Glossary to the END of VISION.md.

WHY (impact MEDIUM-HIGH): skill has ZERO terminology discipline today; jargon and synonym-drift (users/customers/operators) are real vision-doc failures.

ALIGN to domain-modeling skill's CONTEXT.md format (grill-with-docs/CONTEXT-FORMAT.md): '## Language' with **Term**: one-sentence definition (what it IS), _Avoid_: aliases. Opinionated single canonical term.

RESOLUTION ORDER (init + review):
1. If a canonical vocabulary source exists — CONTEXT-MAP.md -> root CONTEXT.md -> glossary/domain model — align VISION.md's load-bearing terms to it (use canonical term, not aliases). Do NOT duplicate it into VISION.md.
2. If NONE exists: add a concise '## Glossary' at the end of VISION.md — load-bearing terms only, one canonical name each, one-sentence definitions, aliases-to-avoid; kept within the <80-line / one-screen budget. Note that a full ubiquitous-language treatment belongs in the domain-modeling skill's CONTEXT.md, and the vision Glossary is a lightweight fallback.
3. Plain language: every load-bearing word defined once; kill jargon.

ACCEPTANCE:
- WORKFLOW.md Init: read-list entry for CONTEXT-MAP.md/CONTEXT.md/domain vocab; resolution-order step.
